skill_question: When you see message stickers like "good morning" or "thank you", can you understand what they mean?
skill_example:
おはよう！
今日もがんばろう！
skill_example_translated:
Good morning!
Let's do our best again today!

skill_question: When you don't know how to say something in Japanese, can you ask how to say it and understand the answer?
skill_example: すみません、これは日本語で何と言いますか？
skill_example_translated: Excuse me, how do you say this in Japanese?

skill_question: Can you fill in your name, nationality, date of birth, and so on, on an application form?
skill_example:
氏名：グエン・バン・アン
国籍：ベトナム
生年月日：1995年3月12日
skill_example_translated:
Name: Nguyen Van Anh
Nationality: Vietnam
Date of birth: March 12, 1995
sentence_example_translated: It's not Vietnam.

skill_question: Can you read a friend's short social media post and, using the photo as a clue, understand what it's about?
skill_example:
海に来ました！
友だちと泳いで、すごく楽しかった〜
skill_example_translated:
We're at the beach!
I swam with my friends and it was so much fun~
sentence_example_translated: This is my mother. This is our pet, John.

skill_question: Can you add a short caption to a photo of your meal and post it on social media?
skill_example: 今日の朝ご飯です。たまごとパンとサラダ。おいしかった！
skill_example_translated: Here's my breakfast today. Eggs, bread, and salad. It was delicious!
sentence_pattern_translated: あまり + verb + ません (frequency)
sentence_example_translated: I don't really eat breakfast.

skill_question: When you see a restaurant's sign, can you tell what kind of restaurant it is?
skill_example:
ラーメン
チャーハン
ぎょうざ
skill_example_translated:
Ramen
Fried rice
Gyoza

skill_question: When you look at the labels for operating home appliances, like an air-conditioner remote, can you tell which button to press?
skill_example:
運転
停止
温度
風量
skill_example_translated:
Start
Stop
Temperature
Fan speed
sentence_pattern_translated: na-adjective + じゃないです / i-adjective + くないです
sentence_example_translated: The apartment isn't very quiet. The dormitory isn't spacious.

skill_question: When you look at the sign at a room's entrance, can you tell what the room is for?
skill_example:
会議室
受付
お手洗い
skill_example_translated:
Meeting room
Reception
Restroom
sentence_pattern_translated: noun + の + (position word) + にあります
sentence_example_translated: It's inside the drawer.

skill_question: Can you ask each other about your availability in order to decide on a schedule?
skill_example: 次の集まりですが、私は土曜日がいいです。みなさんはどうですか？
skill_example_translated: About our next get-together, Saturday works for me. How about everyone else?
sentence_example_translated: Saturday is good for me.

skill_question: Can you look at a list of supplies and check whether everything you need is there?
skill_example:
ボールペン
付箋
ホチキス
コピー用紙
skill_example_translated:
Ballpoint pens
Sticky notes
Stapler
Copy paper
sentence_example_translated: Do you have a phone charger?

skill_question: Can you read a simple social media profile and understand what that person likes?
skill_example: はじめまして。マリアです。料理とK-POPが大好きです。週末は友だちとカラオケに行きます。
skill_example_translated: Nice to meet you. I'm Maria. I love cooking and K-pop. On weekends I go to karaoke with my friends.
sentence_pattern_translated: (person) + と / (place) + で + verb + ます
sentence_example_translated: I play tennis with my husband in the park.

skill_question: Can you read a simple invitation message from a friend and write a reply?
skill_example:
誘ってくれてありがとう！
金曜日の夜、ぜひ行きたいです。何時に集まりますか？
skill_example_translated:
Thanks for inviting me!
I'd love to come on Friday night. What time shall we meet?
sentence_example_translated: Why don't we all go out for yakiniku together?

skill_question: When you see the signs commonly found in stations, can you understand what they mean?
skill_example:
改札口
出口
のりば
切符売り場
skill_example_translated:
Ticket gate
Exit
Boarding area
Ticket counter
sentence_example_translated: I take the train from here to Shin-Minato Station.

skill_question: When you look at notices posted at shops and restaurants, can you understand things like their opening hours and closing days?
skill_example:
営業時間：11:00〜22:00
定休日：毎週水曜日
skill_example_translated:
Hours: 11:00–22:00
Closed: every Wednesday
sentence_pattern_translated: na-adjective + な + noun + ですね / i-adjective + い + noun + ですね
sentence_example_translated: It's a lively street, isn't it. It's a spacious park, isn't it.

skill_question: Can you understand the meaning of signs commonly seen in department stores and shopping centers?
skill_example:
1F 食品
2F 婦人服
3F 子ども服
4F レストラン
skill_example_translated:
1F Food
2F Women's clothing
3F Children's clothing
4F Restaurants
sentence_pattern_translated: na-adjective + ですね / i-adjective + いですね / na-adjective + ！ / i-adjective + い！
sentence_example_translated: This coat is stylish, isn't it. This umbrella is interesting, isn't it. This coat is lovely! This bag is cute!

skill_question: When you see discount labels in a shop, can you tell the price of an item?
sentence_example_translated: I'll have two each of the chocolate cake and the cheesecake, please.

skill_question: Can you send someone you went out with a short message afterward sharing your impressions?
skill_example:
今、家に着きました。今日は楽しかったです！
また行きましょうね。
skill_example_translated:
I just got home. Today was fun!
Let's go again sometime.
sentence_pattern_translated: noun + じゃなかったです / na-adjective + じゃなかったです / i-adjective + くなかったです
sentence_example_translated: I went into a hot spring. But it wasn't an open-air bath. I climbed Mt. Fuji. It wasn't very hard. There weren't very many customers.

skill_question: Can you talk briefly about what you did on a trip and your impressions of it?
skill_example: 先週、京都に行きました。お寺がとてもきれいでした。でも、人が多くて、少し疲れました。
skill_example_translated: Last week I went to Kyoto. The temples were really beautiful. But there were a lot of people, so I got a little tired.
sentence_example_translated: It was delicious. But it was a little expensive.

skill_question: Can you talk briefly about the work you do in Japan?
skill_example: 私は今、レストランで働いています。ホールでお客さんを案内する仕事をしています。
skill_example_translated: Right now I work at a restaurant. My job is to show customers to their seats in the dining area.
sentence_example_translated: What do you do in Japan?

skill_question: Can you write a short staff introduction for posting at work, covering things like your hobbies and what you do on your days off?
skill_example: グエンです。ベトナムから来ました。趣味は料理で、休みの日はよくサッカーをします。
skill_example_translated: I'm Nguyen. I came from Vietnam. My hobby is cooking, and on my days off I often play soccer.
sentence_example_translated: I can speak Japanese, English, Filipino, and Spanish.

skill_question: Can you talk briefly about your favorite season and why you like it?
skill_example: 私は秋が好きです。もみじの景色がきれいですから。それに、果物もおいしいです。
skill_example_translated: I like autumn, because the maple-leaf scenery is beautiful. Also, the fruit is delicious.
sentence_example_translated: Fruit is delicious in autumn, isn't it. Things like grapes and pears.

skill_question: Can you read a short social media post about the weather and understand what it says?
skill_example:
台風が来てます。風がすごく強い！
みなさん、気をつけてください。
skill_example_translated:
A typhoon is coming. The wind is really strong!
Everyone, please take care.

skill_question: Can you look at a town map and read off information about sights, shops, and so on?
skill_example:
中央公園：春は桜が楽しめます
市立美術館：入場無料
skill_example_translated:
Central Park: enjoy the cherry blossoms in spring
City Art Museum: free admission
sentence_pattern_translated: verb + ることができます
sentence_example_translated: You can see all kinds of flowers throughout the year.

skill_question: When someone gives you a ride, can you tell them how to get to your destination?
skill_example: 次の信号を右に曲がってください。この先に白くて大きな家があります。その前で止めてください。
skill_example_translated: Please turn right at the next light. There's a big white house up ahead. Please stop in front of it.
sentence_example_translated: Not the first one — the second one.

skill_question: Can you write a message letting someone know you'll be late for a meetup?
skill_example: ごめんなさい、電車が遅れていて、30分ぐらいおくれます。先に始めていてください。
skill_example_translated: Sorry, the train is delayed, so I'll be about 30 minutes late. Please go ahead and start without me.
sentence_example_translated: I overslept, and I've only just woken up.

skill_question: Can you share with someone your impressions of an event you attended together?
skill_example: 昨日の動物園、楽しかったですね。パンダが本当にかわいかったです。また行きたいです。
skill_example_translated: Yesterday's zoo trip was fun, wasn't it. The pandas were really cute. I'd love to go again.
sentence_example_translated: I'd like to buy some jogging shoes...

skill_question: Can you read an online forum about studying Japanese and understand the recommended study methods?
skill_example: 私のおすすめは、毎日5分でいいので、日本のドラマを見ることです。耳が慣れて、聞き取りが上手になりますよ。
skill_example_translated: My recommendation is to watch Japanese dramas — even just five minutes a day. Your ears get used to it and your listening improves.
sentence_example_translated: I don't really understand how to fill out this form.

skill_question: Before joining a Japanese class, can you answer a simple interview about your learning history and what you'd like to study?
skill_example: 日本語は、高校のとき少し勉強しました。今は、話す練習がしたいです。
skill_example_translated: I studied a little Japanese in high school. Now I'd like to practice speaking.
sentence_example_translated: When I have free time, I cook.

skill_question: Can you look at a food label and check whether it contains anything you can't eat?
skill_example:
名称：クッキー
原材料：小麦粉、砂糖、バター、卵、乳成分
アレルギー：小麦・卵・乳
skill_example_translated:
Name: Cookies
Ingredients: flour, sugar, butter, eggs, milk
Allergens: wheat, egg, milk
sentence_example_translated: A: What do you want to eat? B: Anything is fine.

skill_question: Can you read the preparation instructions on instant food and understand the steps?
skill_example:
①ふたを半分開けて、かやくを入れます。
②お湯を線まで入れて、3分待ちます。
③お湯を捨てて、ソースをかけます。
skill_example_translated:
1. Open the lid halfway and add the seasoning packet.
2. Pour in hot water up to the line and wait 3 minutes.
3. Drain the water and add the sauce.
sentence_example_translated: Isn't the flavor a bit weak?

skill_question: Can you read a simple work-related email and understand its content?
skill_example: お疲れさまです。明日の会議は、9時から第2会議室で行います。資料は当日お配りします。
skill_example_translated: Hello. Tomorrow's meeting will be held from 9:00 in Meeting Room 2. The materials will be handed out on the day.
sentence_pattern_translated: はい、(negative sentence) / いいえ、(affirmative sentence)
sentence_example_translated: A: Have you never used the copier? B: No, I haven't.

skill_question: When you ask how to fill in a form such as a leave request, can you understand the spoken answer?
skill_example: 休暇届はこの用紙です。日付と理由を書いて、上の欄に名前を書いてください。書けたら、私に出してください。
skill_example_translated: The leave-request form is this sheet. Write the date and reason, and put your name in the box at the top. When you're done, hand it to me.
sentence_example_translated: It's that a friend from my home country is coming from Tokyo.

skill_question: Can you read the instructions on medicine and understand how and how much to take?
skill_example:
1日3回、食後に2錠ずつお飲みください。
症状が続くときは、医師にご相談ください。
skill_example_translated:
Take 2 tablets three times a day, after meals.
If symptoms persist, please consult a doctor.
sentence_example_translated: Take this medicine when the pain is too much to bear.

skill_question: When you see a health poster at a hospital or similar place, can you understand things like the symptoms and how to prevent illness?
skill_example:
インフルエンザに気をつけましょう
・手をよく洗う
・マスクをする
・部屋の空気を入れかえる
skill_example_translated:
Let's watch out for the flu
- Wash your hands well
- Wear a mask
- Air out the room
sentence_pattern_translated: verb + ないようにしています
sentence_example_translated: For my health, I try not to overeat.

skill_question: Can you write a short thank-you email to someone who has helped you, such as a person who invited you to their home?
skill_example: 昨日はお招きいただき、ありがとうございました。手料理がとてもおいしかったです。また、お話できるのを楽しみにしています。
skill_example_translated: Thank you for having me yesterday. Your home cooking was delicious. I look forward to talking with you again.
sentence_example_translated: This is a good-luck charm my older brother gave me.

skill_question: Can you write a congratulatory or farewell message on a card or colored paper?
skill_example: ご卒業おめでとうございます。これからの新しい生活が、すてきなものになりますように。
skill_example_translated: Congratulations on your graduation. I hope your new life ahead is wonderful.
sentence_example_translated: I think something more memorable would be better.

skill_question: Can you post a short self-introduction in an online community?
skill_example:
はじめまして！
モンゴルから来たバトです。今、日本語を勉強しています。みなさんと友だちになれたらうれしいです。
skill_example_translated:
Nice to meet you!
I'm Bat, and I came from Mongolia. I'm studying Japanese now. I'd be happy to become friends with everyone.
sentence_pattern_translated: … + て、… / … + から、… / … + けど、… (joining two sentences into one)
sentence_example_translated: There are lots of tea fields, and it's a relaxed place. Ulaanbaatar is the capital, so it's a big city. It isn't the capital, but it's very lively.

skill_question: Can you read a simple interview article introducing someone and understand its content?
skill_example:
――今のお仕事は？
「カフェで働いています。コーヒーをいれるのが得意で、お客さんと話すのも楽しいです。」
skill_example_translated:
— What's your job now?
"I work at a café. I'm good at making coffee, and I also enjoy chatting with customers."
sentence_example_translated: Toshiro Mifune is a good actor, and he's really cool too.

skill_question: Can you look at a restaurant coupon and pick out the information you need?
skill_example:
ランチ20%OFFクーポン
平日11:00〜14:00
他のクーポンとの併用はできません。
skill_example_translated:
Lunch 20% OFF coupon
Weekdays 11:00–14:00
Cannot be combined with other coupons.
sentence_pattern_translated: na-adjective + な / i-adjective + い / verb + る + のは、 + noun + です
sentence_example_translated: The popular one is the sashimi set meal.

skill_question: Can you read online reviews of a restaurant and pick out information like the taste and the prices?
skill_example: ★★★★☆ ラーメンがとてもおいしかったです。値段も安くて、量も多いです。お昼はとても混むので、早めに行くのがおすすめです。
skill_example_translated: ★★★★☆ The ramen was really delicious. It's cheap and the portions are big, too. It gets very crowded at lunch, so I recommend going early.
sentence_example_translated: The restaurant is new and clean, but it's crowded at lunchtime.

skill_question: Can you read reviews on a travel site and understand the general content?
skill_example: 夏に行きましたが、とても暑かったです。朝早く着いたので、ホテルに荷物を預けて、すぐに観光できました。海がきれいで、また行きたいです。
skill_example_translated: I went in summer and it was very hot. I arrived early in the morning, so I left my luggage at the hotel and went sightseeing right away. The sea was beautiful, and I'd like to go again.
sentence_pattern_translated: verb + ないほうがいいです
sentence_example_translated: If you can, it's better not to go on Sundays.

skill_question: Can you post a short account of your travel experiences and impressions on social media?
skill_example:
友だちと2人でハウステンボスに行ってきました！
花がいっぱいで、夜のイルミネーションもきれいでした。最高の一日でした。
skill_example_translated:
I went to Huis Ten Bosch with a friend, just the two of us!
It was full of flowers, and the evening illuminations were beautiful too. It was the best day.
sentence_example_translated: I went to Huis Ten Bosch with a friend, the two of us.

skill_question: When you're invited to a community event, can you ask questions about it and understand the answers?
skill_example:
盆踊りって、何ですか？
だれでも参加できますか？
動きやすい服で行ったほうがいいですか？
skill_example_translated:
What is bon-odori?
Can anyone take part?
Should I go in clothes that are easy to move in?
sentence_example_translated: Bon-odori is easy, so anyone can dance it.

skill_question: Can you write a short post on social media about an event you took part in?
skill_example: 昨日の夏祭りに行きました。屋台がたくさんあって、楽しかったです！
skill_example_translated: I went to the summer festival yesterday. There were lots of food stalls and it was fun!
sentence_example_translated: Do you know what time tomorrow's flea market starts?

skill_question: Can you write and send a New Year's greeting?
skill_example: あけましておめでとうございます。今年もよろしくお願いします。みなさんにとって、いい一年になりますように。
skill_example_translated: Happy New Year. I look forward to your continued friendship this year. I hope it's a good year for everyone.
sentence_example_translated: The date of Lebaran differs from year to year.

skill_question: Can you read online posts about differences in manners and customs and understand the general content?
skill_example: 日本では、電車の中で電話で話さないのがマナーです。最初はびっくりしましたが、今は慣れました。
skill_example_translated: In Japan, it's good manners not to talk on the phone on the train. I was surprised at first, but now I'm used to it.
sentence_example_translated: I have to pay properly before I drink it.

skill_question: Can you listen to an in-store announcement at a place like a shopping mall and understand the general content?
skill_example: お客様にお呼び出しを申し上げます。ベトナムからお越しのグエン様、お連れ様が3階の案内所でお待ちです。
skill_example_translated: We have a message for one of our customers. Mr. Nguyen, visiting from Vietnam — your party is waiting for you at the information desk on the third floor.

skill_question: When buying electronics, can you ask the shop staff about a product and ask them to lower the price?
skill_example:
このドライヤー、もう少し安くなりませんか？
ほかの店では8,000円でした。
skill_example_translated:
Could you make this hairdryer a little cheaper?
It was 8,000 yen at another store.

skill_question: When you see signs like "No Photography" inside a facility, can you understand them?
skill_example:
撮影禁止
飲食禁止
立入禁止
skill_example_translated:
No photography
No eating or drinking
No entry
sentence_example_translated: As for returns, you may return it to any library in the city.

skill_question: At a place like an international exchange association, can you listen and understand what services are available for foreigners?
skill_example: こちらでは、外国の方のために、無料の日本語教室や、生活の相談ができる窓口があります。どうぞお気軽にご利用ください。
skill_example_translated: Here, for foreign residents, we have free Japanese classes and a desk where you can get advice about daily life. Please feel free to use them.
sentence_example_translated: There are various services for foreign residents.

skill_question: Can you ask how to dispose of garbage and understand the answer?
skill_example:
すみません、ペットボトルは、どうやって捨てればいいですか？
ラベルははがしますか？
skill_example_translated:
Excuse me, how should I throw away plastic bottles?
Do I need to peel off the label?
sentence_example_translated: A reusable straw is good for cutting down on waste.

skill_question: Can you read a disaster-prevention pamphlet written in easy Japanese for foreigners and understand its content?
skill_example: 地震のときは、まず火を消してください。そして、机の下に入って、頭を守ってください。
skill_example_translated: When there is an earthquake, first turn off any flames. Then get under a desk and protect your head.
sentence_example_translated: I'm not sure whether the shop is open or not...

skill_question: Can you write and send a short update on how you've been doing to someone like a teacher who taught you Japanese?
skill_example: 先生、お元気ですか。おかげさまで、仕事にも慣れて、日本語で電話もできるようになりました。本当にありがとうございました。
skill_example_translated: Hello sensei, how are you? Thanks to you, I've gotten used to my job and can even make phone calls in Japanese now. Thank you so much.
sentence_pattern_translated: verb + (られ)るようになりました
sentence_example_translated: Lately I've become able to give other people information about the shop.

skill_question: At an event like a farewell party, can you give a thank-you speech that includes a short anecdote?
skill_example: みなさん、今までありがとうございました。入ったばかりのころ、山内さんが優しく仕事を教えてくれました。本当に感謝しています。
skill_example_translated: Everyone, thank you for everything. When I had just joined, Mr. Yamauchi kindly taught me the work. I'm truly grateful.
sentence_example_translated: Mr. Yamauchi taught me a lot of Japanese.
Do you have mayonnaise?

sentence_pattern_translated: noun / na-adjective / i-adjective + い / verb + る + でしょう
sentence_example_translated: It will probably be sunny tomorrow.
I've gone and forgotten my point card.

sentence_pattern_translated: (noun1 + より) + noun2 + のほうが + …
sentence_example_translated: The Toshima one is lighter and easier to move than the Mitsubashi, you know.
If you'd like, won't you come and watch?
On top of that, it's not just games — there are also stage plays, musicals, anime and so on, so there's lots to enjoy. Also, there's variety every time, so it's fun.

sentence_pattern_translated: verb + てもらえたら、うれしいです
sentence_example_translated: If you could do that for me, I'd be really happy.
After all, there are people who are bothered when noise is made late at night.
The thing you get by rolling this up further and deep-frying it in oil is what's called 'dur-ten.'

sentence_pattern_translated: sentence (plain form) + じゃないですか
sentence_example_translated: Eating out costs money — you know how it is, right?

sentence_pattern_translated: verb + ばいいなと思います
sentence_example_translated: I hope I can take part and have fun together with everyone.

sentence_pattern_translated: verb + なくてもだいじょうぶです
sentence_example_translated: You don't have to wear expensive clothes — it's fine.
In the future, I'm hoping I can use what I've studied to do work designing characters for anime and games. The work is hard, but I'm thinking of studying a lot and continuing in caregiving.
I think I've gradually become able to say what I want to say in Japanese.
If you don't carry your residence card, you may have to pay a fine.
I've decided to buy an emergency backpack too, to prepare for disasters.
Today, on behalf of his friends, I will give a congratulatory speech.
I'm troubled about a friend I share a room with.
If I had the time and money, the place I'd really like to visit is the Ogasawara Islands.
We couldn't decide on a place for lunch, and I was made to walk a lot.

sentence_pattern_translated: verb + ていただければありがたいと思います
sentence_example_translated: I'd be very grateful if you could make it possible to do it by email or message and so on.
For example, the other day there was a customer who arrived by taxi on a rainy day. May I look into it a bit more on our end and contact you again?
